z*Help for building DNS wire format messages
Renderera
Helper class for building DNS wire-format messages.
    Most applications can use the higher-level L{dns.message.Message}
    class and its to_wire() method to generate wire-format messages.
    This class is for those applications which need finer control
    over the generation of messages.
    Typical use::
        r = dns.renderer.Renderer(id=1, flags=0x80, max_size=512)
        r.add_question(qname, qtype, qclass)
        r.add_rrset(dns.renderer.ANSWER, rrset_1)
        r.add_rrset(dns.renderer.ANSWER, rrset_2)
        r.add_rrset(dns.renderer.AUTHORITY, ns_rrset)
        r.add_edns(0, 0, 4096)
        r.add_rrset(dns.renderer.ADDITIONAL, ad_rrset_1)
        r.add_rrset(dns.renderer.ADDITIONAL, ad_rrset_2)
        r.write_header()
        r.add_tsig(keyname, secret, 300, 1, 0, '', request_mac)
        wire = r.get_wire()
    output, an io.BytesIO, where rendering is written
    id: the message id
    flags: the message flags
    max_size: the maximum size of the message
    origin: the origin to use when rendering relative names
    compress: the compression table
    section: an int, the section currently being rendered
    counts: list of the number of RRs in each section
    mac: the MAC of the rendered message (if TSIG was used)
    Nr
Initialize a new renderer.Nr
BytesIO
output
randomZ
randint
flags
max_size
origin
compress
QUESTION

selfr
0/usr/lib/python3.9/site-packages/dns/renderer.py
__init__M
Renderer.__init__c
Truncate the output buffer at offset *where*, and remove any
        compression table entries that pointed beyond the truncation
        point.
        N)
seek
truncater
items
append)
whereZ
keys_to_delete
	_rollback^
Renderer._rollbackc
Set the renderer's current section.
        Sections must be rendered order: QUESTION, ANSWER, AUTHORITY,
        ADDITIONAL.  Sections may be empty.
        Raises dns.exception.FormError if an attempt was made to set
        a section value less than the current section.
        N)
	exceptionZ	FormError)
_set_sectionm
Renderer._set_sectionc

Add a question to the message.z

add_question
Renderer.add_questionc
Add the rrset to the specified section.
        Any keyword arguments are passed on to the rdataset's to_wire()
        routine.
        N
rrset
	add_rrset
Renderer.add_rrsetc
Add the rdataset to the specified section, using the specified
        name as the owner name.
        Any keyword arguments are passed on to the rdataset's to_wire()
        routine.
        Nr+

add_rdataset
Renderer.add_rdatasetc

z$Add a TSIG signature to the message.r

	algorithm
keyr<
add_tsig
Renderer.add_tsigc
Add a TSIG signature to the message. Unlike add_tsig(), this can be
        used for a series of consecutive DNS envelopes, e.g. for a zone
        transfer over TCP [RFC2845, 4.4].
        For the first message in the sequence, give ctx=None. For each
        subsequent message, give the ctx that was returned from the
        add_multi_tsig() call for the previous message.r
ctxr@
add_multi_tsig
Renderer.add_multi_tsigc
!HHIHr
Z	rdatatypeZ
TSIG
rdataclass
ANYr$
SEEK_END)
rdata_startZ
afterr
Renderer._write_tsigc
Write the DNS message header.
        Writing the DNS message header is done after all sections
        have been rendered, but before the optional TSIG signature
        is added.
        r
!HHHHHHr
write_header
Renderer.write_headerc
Return the wire format message.)
get_wire
Renderer.get_wire)
